cards.headline: Trump hits Canada with sweeping 50% tariffs
cards.summary: President Trump imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, citing alleged unfair discrimination against US autos, alcohol and dairy products. Canadian Prime Minister Carney vowed to intensify trade talks in response to what marks a major escalation between the two neighbours. The move adds fresh uncertainty to North American trade relations.

quiz.prompt: Why did Trump say he was imposing 50% tariffs on Canadian goods?
options: Canada unfairly discriminated against US autos, alcohol and dairy
Canada refused to join a new North American trade bloc
Canada imposed its own tariffs on US tech exports
Canada blocked a major US infrastructure project
quiz.explanation: Trump officials stated that Canada had unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products as the justification for the 50% tariffs.

cards.headline: Judge freezes Paramount-Warner Bros. merger
cards.summary: A federal judge issued a temporary restraining order halting the proposed Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery merger after 12 state attorneys general sued on antitrust grounds. The $81 billion deal has been paused for at least two weeks while the legal challenge proceeds. States argued the merger would extinguish competition in the media sector.

quiz.prompt: What was the primary legal basis for the states' challenge to the Paramount-Warner merger?
quiz.explanation: The 12 state attorneys general argued the merger would extinguish competition, framing their lawsuit around antitrust law.

cards.headline: Dimon says markets underestimate risks, won't buy stocks
cards.summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon said markets are underestimating current risks and that he would not buy stocks or Treasuries at current prices. His comments contrast sharply with investors' recent willingness to look past wars, tariffs and other economic shocks. The warning from one of Wall Street's most prominent figures is drawing wide attention.

quiz.prompt: What was Jamie Dimon's stated position on stocks and Treasuries at current prices?
options: He would not buy either at current prices
He was bullish on both asset classes
He recommended buying stocks but avoiding Treasuries
quiz.explanation: Dimon explicitly said he would not buy stocks or Treasuries at current prices, citing markets underestimating risks.

cards.headline: US gas and diesel prices climb on Iran tensions
cards.summary: The average price of diesel fuel in the US has risen above $5 a gallon, with regular gasoline approaching $4, nearly $1.25 more than a year ago. Escalating US-Iran tensions and fears over the Strait of Hormuz are driving the increase. Analysts also point to a widening 'crack spread' — the gap between crude oil and refined gasoline prices — as an additional factor pushing pump prices higher.

quiz.prompt: What term describes the spread between crude oil costs and refined gasoline prices that is widening?
quiz.explanation: MarketWatch reported that the 'crack spread' — the difference between the cost of crude oil barrels and gasoline — has widened sharply, contributing to rising pump prices.

cards.headline: Netflix stock falls after disappointing earnings forecast
cards.summary: Netflix shares fell after the company issued an earnings forecast that disappointed investors. The streaming giant also said it would reduce the frequency of its 'What We Watched' engagement reports, which offer a window into viewing habits. The twin disappointments — on outlook and transparency — hit the stock.

quiz.prompt: What additional disclosure change did Netflix announce alongside its disappointing earnings forecast?
options: It would cut back on 'What We Watched' engagement reports
It would stop reporting subscriber numbers quarterly
It would no longer break out regional revenue
It would cease publishing content spending figures
quiz.explanation: Netflix said it would reduce the frequency of its 'What We Watched' reports, which provide a picture of viewer engagement, compounding investor concerns.

cards.headline: GM beats estimates and raises full-year guidance
cards.summary: General Motors reported second-quarter results that beat Wall Street expectations, with revenue growing for the first time in over a year, snapping a four-quarter streak of declines. GM raised several key earnings forecasts, citing a resilient consumer and strong North American operations. The results sent GM's stock higher.

quiz.prompt: What streak did GM's latest quarterly revenue growth snap?
options: Four consecutive quarters of declining revenue
Two years of falling profit margins
Three straight quarters of missing estimates
Six months of flat North American sales
quiz.explanation: MarketWatch reported that GM's revenue growth snapped a four-quarter streak of declines.

cards.headline: Trump Media plans to sell priority access to Truth Social posts
cards.summary: Donald Trump's media company is planning to charge Wall Street trading firms and other institutions for special high-speed access to the president's Truth Social posts before they are widely seen. Critics have denounced the move as brazen corruption, arguing it could allow financial firms to profit from advance knowledge of market-moving statements. The plan raises serious questions about the intersection of presidential communications and financial markets.

quiz.prompt: Who would primarily pay for priority early access to Trump's Truth Social posts under the reported plan?
options: Wall Street trading firms and institutions
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reported the plan would allow Wall Street trading firms and other institutions to pay for high-speed early access to the president's posts.

cards.headline: EU fines AliExpress record €550m for illegal goods
cards.summary: The European Union has fined Chinese retailer AliExpress €550 million — a record penalty — for allowing unsafe toys and counterfeit clothing to be sold on its platform. The fine reflects the EU's toughened stance on policing major online marketplaces under digital market regulations. AliExpress is a platform of Alibaba, one of China's largest technology companies.

quiz.prompt: What types of illegal products led to the EU's record €550m fine against AliExpress?
quiz.explanation: The EU stated that AliExpress allowed unsafe toys and counterfeit clothes to be sold on its site, resulting in the record fine.

cards.headline: UnitedHealth beats estimates, hikes earnings outlook
cards.summary: UnitedHealth Group reported results that blew past analyst estimates and raised its earnings outlook, as the healthcare giant works to stabilise margins. Its strategy includes shrinking membership, exiting unprofitable contracts and investing $1.5 billion in AI. The strong results come as the company seeks to rebuild confidence after a turbulent period.

quiz.prompt: How much is UnitedHealth investing in AI as part of its margin-stabilisation strategy?
quiz.explanation: CNBC reported UnitedHealth is pouring $1.5 billion into AI as part of efforts to stabilise margins alongside shrinking membership and exiting unprofitable contracts.

cards.headline: Thames Water lenders offer 'golden share' to avoid nationalisation
cards.summary: Lenders to Thames Water have proposed a 'golden share' arrangement in an attempt to prevent the Burnham government from nationalising the troubled water supplier. The BBC separately reported that creditors are preparing a legal challenge and would pursue payment in full of outstanding debts running into the multi-billions of pounds if nationalisation proceeds. The standoff represents one of the new UK government's first major financial flashpoints.

quiz.prompt: What financial outcome are Thames Water creditors prepared to pursue if nationalisation goes ahead?
options: Payment in full of all outstanding debts
A discounted settlement with the government
Conversion of debt to equity in a new public entity
An international arbitration claim against the UK
quiz.explanation: The BBC reported that Thames Water creditors would pursue payment in full of the outstanding debts, running into the multi-billions of pounds, in the event of nationalisation.

cards.headline: AliExpress hit with record EU fine for illegal goods
cards.summary: The EU has fined AliExpress €550 million — the largest penalty yet under the Digital Services Act — for allowing unsafe toys and counterfeit clothing to be sold on its platform. The Chinese retailer reportedly failed to make fixes ordered by regulators. AliExpress said it was shocked by the ruling.

quiz.prompt: Why did the EU fine AliExpress a record amount?
options: For allowing unsafe and counterfeit goods on its platform
For failing to pay corporate taxes
quiz.explanation: The EU fined AliExpress for allowing unsafe toys and counterfeit clothes to be sold on its site, the largest fine yet under the Digital Services Act.

cards.headline: China makes a play for global AI regulation leadership
cards.summary: As the world scrambles to regulate artificial intelligence, China's President Xi has called for a stepped-up global effort on AI governance while American-led restrictions continue to block China from accessing advanced technologies. The move is seen as a bid by Beijing to position itself as the leader in setting international AI standards. The tech race between the US and China is intensifying as a result.

quiz.prompt: What is China seeking to do in response to US tech restrictions?
options: Position itself as a global AI governance leader
Partner with US firms on AI standards
Ban Chinese companies from using American AI
quiz.explanation: China's Xi called for a global effort on AI regulation as US curbs squeeze China's tech access, positioning Beijing as a candidate for international AI leadership.

cards.headline: Ransomware surge forces victims into impossible choices
cards.summary: A surge in ransomware attacks is leaving victims — including businesses and institutions — facing the difficult decision of whether to pay attackers or risk losing critical data. Governments are now considering banning ransom payments in response to increasingly sophisticated threats. The debate over payment bans is intensifying as attacks grow more frequent and damaging.

quiz.prompt: What policy measure are governments considering in response to the ransomware surge?
options: Mandating cyber insurance for all firms
Requiring companies to disclose attacks within 24 hours
quiz.explanation: According to the snippet, governments are looking at banning ransom payments in the face of increasingly sophisticated ransomware threats.

cards.headline: Hackers steal data from firm serving US hospitals
cards.summary: Edinburgh-based tech firm Craneware confirmed that a significant amount of customer data was stolen in a cyberattack. The company makes software used by thousands of US hospitals, pharmacies, and clinics for billing patients. The breach raises fresh concerns about the vulnerability of critical healthcare infrastructure to hackers.

quiz.prompt: What does Craneware's software primarily help US hospitals do?
quiz.explanation: Craneware makes software that thousands of US hospitals, pharmacies, and clinics rely on for billing patients.

cards.headline: Critical WordPress flaws put millions of sites at risk
cards.summary: Hackers are actively exploiting two critical security vulnerabilities in WordPress software, potentially allowing them to remotely take over tens of millions of websites. A cybersecurity researcher estimated the scale of sites at risk as significant. The flaws were recently patched, but many sites remain exposed if they have not yet updated.

quiz.prompt: What can hackers do by exploiting the critical WordPress vulnerabilities?
quiz.explanation: The two critical WordPress flaws give hackers the ability to remotely take over tens of millions of websites, according to a cybersecurity researcher.

cards.headline: Judge halts $110B Paramount–Warner Bros. merger
cards.summary: A judge has granted a restraining order blocking Paramount's proposed $110–111 billion purchase of Warner Bros., ruling the deal would likely violate antitrust laws. The lawsuit, brought by US states, alleges the merger would harm movie theaters, basic cable distributors, and audiences. The decision is a significant setback for one of the largest media deals in recent memory.

quiz.prompt: On what grounds did the judge halt the Paramount–Warner Bros. merger?
quiz.explanation: The judge granted a restraining order saying the merger was 'likely to violate antitrust laws,' in a case brought by US states.

cards.headline: AI bots are now texting voters like a candidate would
cards.summary: Political campaigns are increasingly deploying AI bots trained to sound like candidates, sending personalised text messages to engage voters in conversation. The technology makes AI-generated political texting the latest tool campaigns are using to reach constituents. Experts warn this could make political messaging both more effective and more intrusive.

quiz.prompt: How are political campaigns using AI in voter outreach?
options: Deploying bots to send personalised texts mimicking candidates
Using AI to predict election results
Replacing human phone-banking staff with voice AI
quiz.explanation: AI bots trained to sound like candidates are engaging voters in personalised text conversations, making it the latest AI tool used by political campaigns.

cards.headline: Anthropic's landmark $1.5B copyright settlement approved
cards.summary: A court has given final approval to Anthropic's $1.5 billion settlement of a copyright lawsuit over the use of copyrighted works to train its AI models. While the settlement resolves this particular case, it does not address the broader unresolved question of whether using copyrighted material for AI training is legally permissible. The case has been closely watched across the AI industry.

quiz.prompt: What does Anthropic's approved $1.5B settlement leave unresolved?
options: Whether Claude can be used commercially
The broader legality of using copyrighted works to train AI
Whether AI companies must register with copyright authorities
quiz.explanation: The settlement resolves one case but does not settle the wider question of whether using copyrighted material to train AI models is legal.

cards.headline: AI pressure may finally push tech workers to unionise
cards.summary: Tech workers, long resistant to unionisation due to Silicon Valley's culture of individual exceptionalism, are increasingly turning to collective bargaining to contest how companies deploy artificial intelligence. The shift marks a notable change in an industry that has historically avoided organised labour. Concerns about AI-driven job displacement are cited as a key driver.

quiz.prompt: What is driving tech workers to consider unionising, according to the report?
options: Concerns about corporate AI deployment and job security
quiz.explanation: Tech workers are increasingly unionising to contest the corporate deployment of AI, trading Silicon Valley's myth of exceptionalism for collective bargaining.

cards.headline: AI chatbots give inaccurate, unreliable election advice
cards.summary: Research conducted during Hungary's election found that AI chatbots provided inaccurate and inconsistent guidance to voters, including recommending parties that were not even running in the election. The same prompts asked multiple times produced highly volatile and contradictory answers. Experts are raising alarms about voters relying on AI tools for electoral decisions.

quiz.prompt: What did research during Hungary's election find about AI chatbot voting advice?
options: It was accurate but biased toward certain parties
It recommended parties that were not running and gave inconsistent answers
It refused to answer political questions
quiz.explanation: Research showed AI chatbots gave inaccurate guidance including recommending non-running parties, and produced highly volatile answers to identical prompts.

cards.headline: Trump hits Canada with sweeping 50% tariffs
cards.summary: President Trump imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, citing what he called unfair discrimination against American autos, alcohol, and dairy products. Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney responded by vowing to intensify trade talks, signaling Canada will not simply absorb the measures. The move marks a significant escalation in trade tensions between the two neighbouring countries.

quiz.prompt: What reason did the Trump administration give for imposing 50% tariffs on Canadian goods?
options: Canada's unfair treatment of US autos, alcohol, and dairy
Canada's wildfire smoke crossing the border
Canada's refusal to join a defence pact
quiz.explanation: The White House said the tariffs were imposed because Canada unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol, and dairy products.

cards.headline: US-Iran war escalates; diplomats left in limbo
cards.summary: The US launched fresh strikes on Iran, while Iran reported hitting two ships in the Strait of Hormuz and targeting sites in Bahrain and Jordan. Three US service members have now been killed since Friday amid the rising hostilities. Hundreds of US diplomats remain in limbo after the State Department drew down staffing due to Iran's attacks on Gulf states, with it unclear when normal operations will resume.

quiz.prompt: How many US service members have been killed since Friday in the US-Iran conflict?
quiz.explanation: According to NPR, three US service members have been killed since Friday amid rising tensions between the US and Iran.

cards.headline: Hegseth requests $350bn Pentagon funding boost
cards.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth appeared before the Senate seeking a $350 billion funding increase for the Pentagon, as the war with Iran continues with no end in sight. Senator Chris Murphy was among lawmakers speaking out about the escalating Middle East conflict ahead of the testimony. The hearing adds pressure on Congress, which faces a packed agenda before its August recess.

quiz.prompt: How much additional funding did Defense Secretary Hegseth request from Congress?
quiz.explanation: NPR reported that Pete Hegseth went to Capitol Hill seeking a $350 billion funding boost for the Pentagon.

cards.headline: Houthis declare maritime embargo on Saudi Arabia
cards.summary: Yemen's Houthi movement announced an immediate maritime embargo on Saudi Arabia, calling it a response to a Saudi blockade of Yemeni ports and airports. The Houthis, aligned with Iran, also separately threatened a naval blockade of the Bab Al-Mandeb Strait, a critical Red Sea waterway. The moves raise fresh concerns about disruption to global shipping lanes already strained by the broader US-Iran conflict.

quiz.prompt: What reason did the Houthis give for declaring a maritime embargo on Saudi Arabia?
options: In solidarity with Palestinians in Gaza
In response to a Saudi blockade of Yemeni ports and airports
To support Iran's strikes on Gulf states
To protest US military operations in Yemen
quiz.explanation: The Houthis stated that their embargo was a direct response to Saudi Arabia's blockade of ports and airports in Yemen.

cards.headline: India's 'Cockroach' protest movement defies crackdown
cards.summary: India's youth-led protest movement, which calls itself the 'cockroaches,' vowed to keep campaigning after a violent police crackdown in Delhi left at least 60 protesters and 118 officers injured. Leaders said they would halt further marches on parliament but continue other forms of agitation, having evolved from a satirical movement into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Modi's government. The protests centre on demands for education reform and action against corruption amid a shortage of opportunities for young people.

quiz.prompt: What did India's 'Cockroach' protest leaders decide after the police crackdown in Delhi?
options: They called off all protest activity entirely
They escalated to a general strike
They said they would keep campaigning but halt further marches on parliament
They announced plans to form a formal political party
quiz.explanation: Leaders of the movement said they would continue campaigning but would not stage another march through New Delhi following the violent crackdown.

cards.headline: Ortega declares Nicaragua will hold no more elections
cards.summary: Nicaraguan President Daniel Ortega, the 80-year-old authoritarian leader, announced that the country will hold no further elections, saying the move would 'build a wall' against the opposition. The declaration represents a stark step toward entrenching one-party rule in the Central American nation. The announcement is expected to draw international condemnation from democratic governments.

quiz.prompt: What reason did Nicaragua's President Ortega give for ending elections?
options: To focus resources on economic development
To 'build a wall' against the opposition
To align with a new regional security pact
To comply with a new constitutional amendment
quiz.explanation: Ortega said the decision to hold no further elections would 'build a wall' against the opposition, according to the Guardian.

cards.headline: Lebanese army deploys as Israel begins partial withdrawal
cards.summary: Lebanon's army announced troops are deploying in a 'pilot zone' following a US-brokered agreement that should see Israeli forces pulling out of a small occupied part of southern Lebanon. Lebanese President Joseph Aoun travelled to Washington to meet President Trump, seeking US backing to end the fighting and secure a full Israeli withdrawal. The partial pullback represents the first concrete movement under the ceasefire framework.

quiz.prompt: What brokered the agreement for Israeli troops to begin withdrawing from part of Lebanon?
quiz.explanation: BBC reported that a US-brokered agreement underpins the partial Israeli withdrawal from occupied southern Lebanon.

cards.headline: 'El Mayo' Zambada sentenced to life in US prison
cards.summary: Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada, co-founder of the Sinaloa Cartel, was sentenced to life in a US prison after admitting to sending vast quantities of drugs into the United States. He was also ordered to pay $15 billion as part of the sentencing. Zambada is one of the most notorious drug traffickers ever to face US justice.

quiz.prompt: How much was Sinaloa Cartel co-founder 'El Mayo' Zambada ordered to pay alongside his life sentence?
quiz.explanation: BBC reported that Zambada was ordered to pay $15 billion in addition to receiving a life sentence.

cards.headline: Burnham's new UK cabinet cuts electricity bill taxes
cards.summary: Newly installed UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham announced his cabinet and moved quickly to cut taxes on electricity bills, fulfilling a cost-of-living pledge. World leaders offered congratulations while global media focused on the scale of challenges he faces. The EU, meanwhile, signalled cautious optimism about relations with the new PM, though analysts note he faces the same structural constraints as his predecessor on trade.

quiz.prompt: What domestic economic measure did UK PM Andy Burnham announce shortly after taking office?
options: Cuts to taxes on electricity bills
A reduction in the national insurance rate
quiz.explanation: Al Jazeera reported that Burnham announced cuts to taxes on electricity bills as part of his early action on the cost-of-living crisis.

cards.headline: Healthy diet unaffordable for 2.69 billion people globally
cards.summary: A UN report found that nearly 2.69 billion people worldwide cannot afford a healthy diet, even as global hunger declined for the third consecutive year in 2025. Experts called for urgent action to bring down the cost of fruit, vegetables, and dairy products. The report highlights that hunger, while falling, remains higher than pre-COVID levels.

quiz.prompt: How many people globally cannot afford a healthy diet, according to the UN report?
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reported that the UN found 2.69 billion people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, despite a third consecutive year of declining global hunger.

cards.headline: Putin meets North Korean FM in rare diplomatic push
cards.summary: North Korean Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui visited Moscow for talks with President Putin in a visit that lacked an obvious diplomatic occasion such as a multilateral summit or state anniversary. The unusual timing has raised speculation about deeper coordination between the two countries. The meeting comes as Russia continues its war in Ukraine and faces broad Western sanctions.

quiz.prompt: What made North Korean FM Choe Son Hui's visit to Moscow notable, according to NPR?
options: It coincided with a major Russian state anniversary
It came without an obvious occasion such as a multilateral event
It was her first ever visit to Russia
It included a formal defence treaty signing
quiz.explanation: NPR noted that the visit came without an obvious occasion like a multilateral diplomatic event or state anniversary, raising speculation about its purpose.

cards.headline: FIFA opens probe into World Cup final brawls
cards.summary: FIFA has confirmed it is investigating the violent scenes that followed Spain's World Cup final victory over Argentina. Leandro Paredes was shown a red card after the final whistle, and FIFA is also looking into a Falklands banner displayed during an earlier England match. Argentina players could face bans as a result of the inquiry.

quiz.prompt: What action is FIFA taking following the ugly scenes at the World Cup final?
options: Replaying the match behind closed doors
Opening an investigation that could result in Argentina player bans
Fining the Spanish FA for provocation
Suspending the referee who officiated the final
quiz.explanation: FIFA confirmed it has opened an investigation into the violent post-match scenes, with Argentina players potentially facing bans.

cards.headline: Messi says 'pain is immense' after final defeat
cards.summary: Lionel Messi said 'the pain is immense' and that 'it will take time to heal' after Argentina lost 1-0 to Spain in the World Cup final. Spain celebrated their triumph with an open-top bus parade through Madrid attended by thousands of fans. UEFA president Aleksander Ceferin notably boycotted the final in protest against FIFA.

quiz.prompt: Why did UEFA president Aleksander Ceferin not attend the World Cup final?
options: He was attending another sporting event
He boycotted it in protest against FIFA
He was ill and could not travel
He was not invited by the host nation
quiz.explanation: According to the reports, Ceferin boycotted the World Cup final as a protest against FIFA.

cards.headline: Kevin Keegan dies aged 75
cards.summary: Kevin Keegan, the former England manager and twice-named European Footballer of the Year, has died aged 75. He had revealed in June 2026 that he had stage four cancer. Keegan was celebrated for his playing career at Liverpool, Hamburg and Newcastle, as well as his passionate management style.

quiz.prompt: How old was Kevin Keegan when he died?
quiz.explanation: Reports confirm that Kevin Keegan died aged 75.

cards.headline: Hamilton vows to 'step it up' at Hungarian GP
cards.summary: Lewis Hamilton has said he needs to 'step it up' at this weekend's Hungarian Grand Prix after a difficult Belgian GP in which he crashed and received a penalty. Hamilton admitted he 'can't afford' to repeat the Spa setbacks amid Ferrari's competitive title fight. Questions remain over whether his penalty was fair following contact with teammate George Russell.

quiz.prompt: At which circuit does Lewis Hamilton say he needs to improve following his Belgian GP setbacks?
quiz.explanation: Hamilton specifically said he needs to 'step it up' at the Hungarian Grand Prix after his troubles in Belgium.

cards.headline: DeChambeau accused of threatening to call Trump
cards.summary: Bryson DeChambeau has been accused of threatening to call Donald Trump during a row over the two-stroke penalty he received at The Open Championship. The incident added controversy to an already fiery week at Birkdale, where Ryan Fox ultimately won his first major title. DeChambeau has not publicly confirmed the allegation.

quiz.prompt: What penalty did Bryson DeChambeau receive at The Open Championship?
quiz.explanation: Reports state DeChambeau was given a two-stroke penalty during his second round at The Open.

cards.headline: Angel Reese accepts coach's apology over comment
cards.summary: Angel Reese has accepted Toronto Tempo coach Sandy Brondello's apology after Brondello made an inappropriate comment during a Friday game that landed her a ban. The term used is described as common in Australia but could be seen as racist in other contexts. The WNBA had already suspended Brondello over the remark.

quiz.prompt: Which WNBA coach apologised to Angel Reese after making an inappropriate comment?
quiz.explanation: Toronto Tempo coach Sandy Brondello apologised to Angel Reese after her comment resulted in a ban.

cards.headline: Arsenal, Chelsea, Man Utd chase Scott after contract rejection
cards.summary: Bournemouth midfielder Alex Scott has rejected a new contract offer from the club, with Arsenal, Chelsea and Manchester United all understood to be interested in signing the England Under-21 international. The 21-year-old's decision to turn down a new deal sets up a potential transfer battle among the Premier League's biggest clubs. Scott's future is expected to be resolved before the close of the summer window.

quiz.prompt: Which club does Alex Scott currently play for after rejecting their new contract offer?
quiz.explanation: Alex Scott rejected a new contract at Bournemouth, prompting interest from several top Premier League clubs.

cards.headline: Menzies stuns world No. 2 Humphries at World Matchplay
cards.summary: Cameron Menzies produced a major upset at the World Matchplay darts, defeating 2024 champion and world number two Luke Humphries 10-7. Menzies described the win as 'the biggest moment of my darts career.' Analyst Wayne Mardle said the victory means Menzies now knows he can handle anything at the top level.

quiz.prompt: What was the score when Cameron Menzies defeated Luke Humphries at the World Matchplay?
quiz.explanation: Menzies defeated Humphries 10-7 in the World Matchplay first round.

cards.headline: Fake football shirts worth £18m seized in UK
cards.summary: UK police seized counterfeit football shirts worth over £18m during the course of the World Cup due to safety concerns. The Fire Service warned that the fake outfits can pose a danger to wearers. The seizure is one of the largest of its kind linked to a major football tournament.

quiz.prompt: Why did the UK Fire Service warn about the seized counterfeit football shirts?
options: They can be a danger to those who wear them
They were made in unsafe factories
quiz.explanation: The Fire Service warned that the counterfeit shirts can be a danger to those who wear them, which was the stated safety concern behind the seizure.

cards.headline: Cummins, Hazlewood and Lyon return for Bangladesh Tests
cards.summary: Australia have received a major boost ahead of their two-Test series against Bangladesh, with Pat Cummins, Josh Hazlewood and Nathan Lyon all confirmed fit to return to the squad. The three bowlers had been unavailable but are now back to bolster Australia's attack. Their returns significantly strengthen Australia's bowling options for the series.

quiz.prompt: How many Tests will Australia play against Bangladesh upon the return of their key bowlers?
quiz.explanation: The reports confirm Australia will play a two-Test series against Bangladesh.

cards.headline: Trump hits Canada with sweeping 50% tariffs
cards.summary: President Trump has imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, citing unfair discrimination against US autos, alcohol and dairy products. Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney vowed to intensify trade talks in response to what marks a major escalation between the two neighbours. Reports suggest Trump is also exploring additional tariffs on Canada related to wildfire smoke impacts in the US.

quiz.prompt: Why did Trump say he was imposing 50% tariffs on Canada?
options: Canada unfairly discriminated against US autos, alcohol and dairy
Canada imposed its own tariffs on US tech companies
Canada refused to join a new North American trade agreement
quiz.explanation: The White House stated that Canada had unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products as the justification for the tariffs.

cards.headline: US launches 10th round of Iran strikes
cards.summary: The US military has launched its 10th consecutive round of strikes on Iran, with Trump vowing retaliation after the deaths of US soldiers. Iran declared the situation a 'full-scale war' while also claiming to have hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz and struck targets in Bahrain and Jordan. Diplomats say de-escalation talks are continuing even as Democrats call on Trump to end the military campaign.

quiz.prompt: What did Iran claim to have struck alongside its declaration of full-scale war?
options: Two ships in the Strait of Hormuz and targets in Bahrain and Jordan
A US naval base in the Persian Gulf
quiz.explanation: Iran said it hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan during the latest round of exchanges.

cards.headline: Houthis threaten blockade of key Red Sea strait
cards.summary: Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen have announced a naval blockade of the Bab Al-Mandeb Strait, a critical waterway for global shipping on the Red Sea. The announcement comes amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict, with the Houthis aligned with Tehran. The move threatens to further disrupt international maritime trade through one of the world's busiest shipping corridors.

quiz.prompt: Which waterway have the Houthis announced they will blockade?
quiz.explanation: The Houthis announced a naval blockade of the Bab Al-Mandeb Strait, a key Red Sea waterway for global shipping.

cards.headline: Lebanese army deploys as Israeli troops begin pullout
cards.summary: Lebanon's army has begun deploying troops in a pilot zone following a US-brokered agreement that should see Israeli forces pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah. Lebanese President Aoun is in Washington seeking US backing to end the fighting and secure a broader Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon. The diplomatic push marks a cautious step toward stabilising the border region.

quiz.prompt: What brokered the agreement for Israeli forces to begin pulling out of Lebanon?
quiz.explanation: According to reports, the agreement for Israeli troops to pull out of a small part of Lebanon was US-brokered.

cards.headline: UK cuts VAT on household electricity bills in October
cards.summary: New UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham announced a VAT cut on household electricity bills, which is expected to save homes around £45 a year and take effect in October. The move was made on Burnham's first full day as prime minister as part of efforts to tackle the cost-of-living crisis. However, former ally Darren Jones criticised the policy as unfunded, signalling early tensions within Labour.

quiz.prompt: Approximately how much will UK households save annually from the electricity VAT cut?
quiz.explanation: The BBC reported that homes will save around £45 a year through the VAT cut on electricity bills.

cards.headline: FIFA investigates Argentina over World Cup final trouble
cards.summary: FIFA has confirmed it is opening an investigation into Argentina following ugly scenes at the end of the World Cup final. Hollywood actor Samuel L Jackson was among those who criticised Argentina, with racism among the allegations raised. The post-match controversy has overshadowed Spain's victory and sparked widespread debate about conduct at the tournament.

quiz.prompt: What prompted FIFA to open an investigation into Argentina after the World Cup final?
options: Ugly scenes and allegations including racism after the final
Argentina's manager disputing the referee's decisions
quiz.explanation: FIFA confirmed an investigation into Argentina following ugly scenes at the end of the final, with racism among the allegations raised.

cards.headline: Parts of England edge closer to drought conditions
cards.summary: Eight areas across England are now in prolonged dry weather, the stage immediately before official drought status is declared. Thames Water announced a hosepipe ban affecting 10.1 million customers, attributing the decision to three successive heatwaves. A devastating wildfire has also been burning in Scotland's Cairngorms for seven days, with helicopters working to contain the spread.

quiz.prompt: How many customers does the Thames Water hosepipe ban affect?
quiz.explanation: Thames Water announced a hosepipe ban affecting 10.1 million customers, citing three heatwaves as a contributing factor.

cards.headline: India's 'Cockroach' movement pauses marches after crackdowns
cards.summary: India's youth-led Cockroach protest movement, which began as a satirical demonstration, has halted further marches through New Delhi following violent police action that injured more than 100 students. Leaders say they will continue campaigning but will not stage another march on parliament for now. The movement has grown into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.

quiz.prompt: What triggered the Cockroach movement to pause its marches on parliament?
options: Police violence that injured more than 100 students
Rain and flooding in New Delhi
quiz.explanation: Movement leaders said they would not stage another march after police action injured more than 100 students during demonstrations.

cards.headline: Russian drone strike sets Ukraine shopping centre ablaze
cards.summary: A large fire engulfed a shopping centre in Ukraine's Sumy city after Russian drone strikes hit the building. The attack is the latest in a continuing campaign of Russian strikes on Ukrainian civilian infrastructure. The strike adds to growing international concern over the targeting of non-military sites.

quiz.prompt: Which Ukrainian city's shopping centre was set ablaze by Russian drone strikes?
quiz.explanation: Al Jazeera reported that a fire engulfed a shopping centre in Ukraine's Sumy following Russian drone strikes on the building.

cards.headline: Russian warship fires live rounds near UK coast
cards.summary: A Russian warship carried out a live-fire military exercise approximately 46 miles off the British coast, according to the UK Ministry of Defence. The incident raises fresh concerns about Russian naval activity in waters close to the United Kingdom. No further details about the nature of the weapons used or the vessel involved were immediately provided.

quiz.prompt: How far off the British coast did the Russian warship conduct its live-fire exercise?
quiz.explanation: The UK Ministry of Defence said the Russian warship carried out the exercise around 46 miles off the British coast.
